name: BROKEN TOP CLUB - GOLF MAINT
address: 367 SW MT WASHINGTON DR, BEND, OR 97702

fr.elements: Main Building; Vehicle Parking Area; Landscaped Area; Detached Structure
fr.recommendations: Ensuring that the various access points to the property are clearly marked and easily distinguishable may facilitate more efficient navigation and quicker response by emergency personnel when attending to incidents. Establishing consistent and unobstructed routes between the multiple structures may assist responders in moving swiftly between different areas of the property. Explicit signage and lighting at night near the vehicle parking areas could enhance visibility and assist in guiding responders to their staging positions. Additionally, maintaining clear pathways around the landscaped area and ensuring it does not encroach on access routes may support maneuverability and streamline movement during emergency operations. Reducing visual obstructions around the main and detached structures could further aid visibility and situate responders optimally for effective emergency management.

wildfire.conditions: Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Scattered Trees; Dense Tree Canopy; Vegetation Adjacent to Access Route; Open Fuel Break; Vegetated Perimeter; Tree Cluster; Limited Defensible Space
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space by managing vegetation immediately adjacent to the structure and increasing separation between vegetation and the building could improve wildfire resilience. Reducing the density of the canopy and managing scattered trees could decrease fuel continuity, mitigates potential crown fire spread, and supports long-term vegetation management. Addressing vegetation near access routes may bolster firefighter access and suppression operations. Maintaining and augmenting the open fuel break could serve as an effective barrier to fire movement and should be preserved as part of an ongoing vegetation management strategy. Enhancing treatments around the tree cluster and along the vegetated perimeter could decrease fuel loads, reducing the likelihood of fire spread toward the structure. Additionally, increasing defensible space by expanding open areas around the residence and preserving this zone over time could further support property preparedness and enhance overall protection.
